title: Ruellia infundibuliformis
intro: Ruellia infundibuliformis, commonly known as the Mexican petunia or wild petunia, is a flowering plant species native to Mexico and Central America. The plant is prized for its medicinal properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ruellia infundibuliformis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to be responsible for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory problems.
